title: Acquisition and tracking apparatus for free space optical communications
abstract: A method (900) includes receiving a first optical signal (320, 320a) at a first communication terminal (302, 302a) from a second communication terminal (302, 302b) through a free space optical link (322). The received optical signal contains a modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b). The method also includes mixing the modulated unique frequency tone with a reference signal (411) to provide a mixed output signal (413) and determining a signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone based on the mixed output signal. The reference signal includes a same frequency as the modulated unique frequency tone. The method adjusts an optical head (310, 310a, 310b) of the first communication terminal to establish acquisition and optical beam pointing with the second communication terminal based on the signal strength of the modulated unique frequency tone received from the second communication terminal.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method (900) comprising: receiving, at a first communication terminal (302, 302a), an optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from a second communication terminal (302, 302b) through a free space optical link (322), the received optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) containing a modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a); mixing, by control hardware (800, 800a, 800b) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a), the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with a reference signal (411) to provide a mixed output signal (413), the reference signal (411) comprising a same frequency as the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b); determining, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), a signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) based on the mixed output signal (413); and adjusting, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), an optical head (310, 310a, 310b) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to establish acquisition and optical beam pointing with the second communication terminal (302, 302b) based on the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 2. The method (900) of claim 1, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) combines a data signal (321) comprising one or more data packets (321a-n) and a beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) modulated at the unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) onto a multiplexed optical carrier comprising the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) for transmission to the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 3. The method (900) of claim 2, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) uses one of a wavelength division multiplexer (406) or a polarization multiplexer (406) to combine the data signal (321) and the beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) onto the multiplexed optical carrier. 4. The method (900) of claim 2, further comprising: 

 when the first communication terminal (302, 302a) receives the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from the second communication terminal (302, 302b): de-multiplexing, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), the received optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) into the data signal (321) and the beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b), wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises mixing the received beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) from a local oscillator (412) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to provide the mixed output signal (413). 5. The method (900) of claim 1, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) combines the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) onto a modulated data signal (321) containing a stream of data packets (321a-n) to produce the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) for transmission to the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 6. The method (900) of claim 5, wherein the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises a lower frequency than a frequency of the modulated data signal (321). 7. The method (900) of claim 5, wherein the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises a smaller power modulation depth than the modulated data signal (321). 8. The method (900) of claim 5, further comprising: when the first communication terminal (302, 302a) receives the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from the second communication terminal (302, 302b): splitting, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) into the modulated data signal (321) and the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b); and 

 encoding, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), the stream of data packets (321a-n) on the modulated data signal (321), wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) from a local oscillator (412) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to provide the mixed output signal (413). 9. The method (900) of any of claims 1-8, wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) eliminates noise from the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) when the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) is received by the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 10. The method (900) of any of claims 1-9, wherein determining the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises: filtering the mixed output signal (413) to extract a direct-current component from the mixed output signal (413); and determining the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) based on a value of the direct-current component extracted from the mixed output signal (413). 11. The method (900) of any of claims 1-10, wherein the adjusting the optical head (310, 310a, 310b) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) establishes the acquisition and optical beam pointing with the second communication terminal (302, 302b) by increasing the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 12. The method (900) of any of claims 1-11, further comprising: after optical beam acquisition and pointing is established between the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b): 

 transmitting a first telemetry signal (720, 720a) from the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the first telemetry signal (720, 720a) informing the second communication terminal (302, 302b) to reduce a divergence angle of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) during subsequent transmissions to the first communication terminal (302, 302a) through the optical link (322); and receiving, at the first communication terminal (302, 302a), a second telemetry signal (720, 720b) from second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the second telemetry signal (720, 720b) informing the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to reduce a divergence angle of optical beam transmissions to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322). 13. The method (900) of any of claims 1-11, further comprising: after optical beam acquisition and pointing is established between the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b): transmitting a first telemetry signal (720, 720a) from the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the first telemetry signal (720, 720a) informing the second communication terminal (302, 302b) to redirect a transmitting angle of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) during subsequent transmissions to the first communication terminal (302, 302a) through the optical link (322); and receiving, at the first communication terminal (302, 302a), a second telemetry signal (720, 720b) from second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the second telemetry signal (720, 720b) informing the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to redirect a transmitting angle of optical beam transmissions to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322). 14. The method (900) of any of claims 1-13, further comprising: 

 determining, by the controller hardware, a received power (725, 725a-n) of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b) at each one of three or more photodetectors (702, 702a-n) associated with receiving optics (308) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a); determining, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), whether the received power (725, 725a-n) at each of the photodetectors (702, 702a-n) is balanced; and when the received power (725, 725a-n) at the photodetectors (702, 702a-n) is unbalanced, adjusting, by the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), the pointing of the receiving optics (308) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) until the receiving optics (308) are centered with the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 15. The method (900) of any of claims 1-14, wherein the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b) are stationary optical terminals. 16. A first communication terminal (302, 302a) comprising: an optical head (310, 310a, 310b) comprising transmitter optics (306, 306a, 306b) and receiving optics (308), the receiving optics (308) configured to receive an optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from a second communication terminal (302, 302b) through a free space optical link (322), the received optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) containing a modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b); control hardware (800, 800a, 800b) in communication with the optical head (310, 310a, 310b); memory hardware (802) in communication with the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b), the memory hardware (802) storing instructions that when executed on the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b) cause the control hardware (800, 800a, 800b) to perform operations comprising: mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with a reference signal (411) to provide a mixed output signal (413), the reference signal (411) comprising a same frequency as the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b); 

 determining a signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) based on the mixed output signal (413); and adjusting an optical head (310, 310a, 310b) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to establish acquisition and optical beam pointing with the second communication terminal (302, 302b) based on the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 17. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 16, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) combines a data signal (321) comprising one or more data packets (321a-n) and a beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) modulated at the unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) onto a multiplexed optical carrier comprising the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) for transmission to the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 18. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 17, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) uses one of a wavelength division multiplexer (406) or a polarization multiplexer (406) to combine the data signal (321) and the beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) onto the multiplexed optical carrier. 19. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 17, wherein the operations further comprise: when the first communication terminal (302, 302a) receives the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from the second communication terminal (302, 302b): de-multiplexing the received optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) into the data signal (321) and the beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b), wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises mixing the received beacon signal (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) from a local oscillator (412) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to provide the mixed output signal (413). 20. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 16, wherein the second communication terminal (302, 302b) combines the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) onto a modulated data signal (321) containing a stream of data packets (321a-n) to produce the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) for transmission to the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 21. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 20, wherein the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises a lower frequency than a frequency of the modulated data signal (321). 22. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 20, wherein the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a) comprises a smaller power modulation depth than the modulated data signal (321). 23. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of claim 20, wherein the operations further comprise: when the first communication terminal (302, 302a) receives the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) from the second communication terminal (302, 302b): splitting the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) into the modulated data signal (321) and the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b); and encoding the stream of data packets (321a-n) on the modulated data signal (321), wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) from a local oscillator (412) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to provide the mixed output signal (413). 24. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-24, wherein mixing the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) with the reference signal (411) eliminates noise from the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) when 

 the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) is received by the first communication terminal (302, 302a). 25. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-24, wherein determining the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) comprises: filtering the mixed output signal (413) to extract a direct-current component from the mixed output signal (413); and determining the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) based on a value of the direct-current component extracted from the mixed output signal (413). 26. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-25, wherein the adjusting the optical head (310, 310a, 310b) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) establishes the acquisition and optical beam pointing with the second communication terminal (302, 302b) by increasing the signal strength (415) of the modulated unique frequency tone (318, 318a, 318b) of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 27. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-26, wherein the operations further comprise: after optical beam acquisition and pointing is established between the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b): transmitting a first telemetry signal (720, 720a) from the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the first telemetry signal (720, 720a) informing the second communication terminal (302, 302b) to reduce a divergence angle of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) during subsequent transmissions to the first communication terminal (302, 302a) through the optical link (322); and 

 receiving a second telemetry signal (720, 720b) at the first communication terminal (302, 302a) from second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the second telemetry signal (720, 720b) informing the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to reduce a divergence angle of optical beam transmissions to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322). 28. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-26, wherein the operations further comprise: after optical beam acquisition and pointing is established between the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b): transmitting a first telemetry signal (720, 720a) from the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the first telemetry signal (720, 720a) informing the second communication terminal (302, 302b) to redirect a transmitting angle of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) during subsequent transmissions to the first communication terminal (302, 302a) through the optical link (322); and receiving, at the first communication terminal (302, 302a), a second telemetry signal (720, 720b) from the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322), the second telemetry signal (720, 720b) informing the first communication terminal (302, 302a) to redirect a transmitting angle of optical beam transmissions to the second communication terminal (302, 302b) through the optical link (322). 29. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-28, wherein the operations further comprise: determining a received power (725, 725a-n) of the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b) at each one of three or more photodetectors (702, 702a-n) associated with receiving optics (308) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a); 

 determining whether the received power (725, 725a-n) at each of the photodetectors (702, 702a-n) is balanced; and when the received power (725, 725a-n) at the photodetectors (702, 702a-n) is unbalanced, adjusting the pointing of the receiving optics (308) of the first communication terminal (302, 302a) until the receiving optics (308) are centered with the optical signal (320, 320a, 320b) received from the second communication terminal (302, 302b). 30. The first communication terminal (302, 302a) of any of claims 16-29, wherein the first communication terminal (302, 302a) and the second communication terminal (302, 302b) are stationary optical terminals.
